scenes.sceneDesc: Oz. Past. A green cyclone drops a basket besides a yellow-brick road. In the distance, the Emerald City can be seen. A woodcutter and his wife are walking along the road. A baby can be heard wailing.

dialog.dialog: Did you hear that?
Nothing. Stick to the road.
Oh, where did it come from?
The cyclone must have dropped it. Dear, what are you doing?
What do you expect me to do? Leave the poor thing to the wolves? She's beautiful.
She did that. She knocked that tree away.
Calm your nerves, dear. It was just the wind. She's only a babe and she needs our help. Can we keep her?
That's not a good idea. Didn't you see what she did? She's not like us.
You're right, she's not. She's alone and we’re going to take care of her. Now, stick to the road or we'll never make it to the Emerald City before dawn.
What should we call her?
What about Zelena? She's going to be the happiest baby in all of Oz.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Zelena's Farmhouse. Zelena walks up to Mr. Gold’s cage.
Storybrooke. Present day. Zelena's Farmhouse. Zelena walks up to Mr. Gold’s cage.

dialog.dialog: You've spent so long figuring out how to get to this land, groomed Regina to cast your curse, spent twenty-eight years waiting for it to be broken, all that you can be with your son. Now, he's gone. Tell me, Rumple, was he really worth all that trouble?
Every bit of it. He was family. Something you know nothing about.

scenes.sceneDesc: OZ. Past. Zelena, now a young adult, is helping her father to shave.
OZ. Past. Zelena, now a young adult, is helping her father to shave.

dialog.dialog: How's that, father?
You've missed a spot.  No matter, how you feel on the inside, Zelena, remember to always put on a good face.
I know, father.
Careless child.  Don't touch me!
I'm sorry. I can't help it.
You mean you can't control it.
Maybe if you'd let me learn how to—
Then everyone would see what you really are. Wicked.
How can you say that to your own daughter?
Because you're not my daughter! There, I’ve said it. After all these years your mother's gone now I can finally tell you the truth.
What's it you're talking about?

You're not our child, Zelena. We found you in the woods. In a basket. Dropped out of a tornado. Your mother wanted to take you in, she was lulled by your beauty. But under the surface there was something else—your wickedness.
I'd rather be wicked than a sad old drunk.
What do you think drove me to drink, child? Wipe away those tears and put on a good face! Make me breakfast.
I think not. If I really caused you this much trouble, I'll spare you any more.
Where do you think you're going?
To see the one person who can help me find a family who wants me! The Wizard of Oz.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Granny's Diner. Emma is throwing darts as every single one hits the bull's eye. Hook approaches her.
Storybrooke. Present day. Granny's Diner. Emma is throwing darts as every single one hits the bull's eye. Hook approaches her.

dialog.dialog: Perhaps I should paint a bull's eye on the Wicked Witch's back.
She'll get more than a dart when I find her.
I know you're hurting, Swan, but there are better ways to grieve Baelfire’s death than letting anger overcome you.
Let me guess—rum?
Never hurts.
I'll stick with anger. At least, until I’ve dealt with Zelena.
Take it from me: Vengeance isn't the thing that's gonna make you feel better.
It'll make this town safer.  Besides, I promised Henry I'd find the person responsible for his father's death. It's really all I can do for him right now.
That can't be true. Have you tried talking to the boy?
As far as he is concerned, I haven't seen Neal since he left me in jail. So anything I'd tell him about his dad being a hero sounds like I'm making that up just to get him to feel better.
Perhaps I can talk to him.
About what? Leather conditioner and eyeliner?
I knew Bae as a boy. Perhaps Henry’d like to hear what his father was like when he was his age.
You'd really do that?
Aye. Could help the boy make peace with his father's passing. And me.
Be careful. Zelena's still out there.
I assure you, nothing will happen to the boy while he's in my charge.
Henry, do you remember Killian?
Hello, Henry.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Elsewhere in Granny’s Diner. From a distance, Regina watches Robin Hood ordering drinks. Tinker Bell enters the diner. As Tinker Bell walks past Robin, she notices the lion tattoo on his arm.

Storybrooke. Present day. Elsewhere in Granny’s Diner. From a distance, Regina watches Robin Hood ordering drinks. Tinker Bell enters the diner. As Tinker Bell walks past Robin, she notices the lion tattoo on his arm.

dialog.dialog: It's the lion tattoo. It's what was prophesied. He's your soul mate.
I know. I saw it yesterday.
And you didn't tell me?
Well, right now I have better things to do than gossip about boys!
Care for a drink?  I do hope I didn't upset you yesterday.
Robin Hood, Tinker Bell. Tinker Bell, Robin Hood.
I’ve been wanting to meet you for a very long time.
And no, you didn't upset me. I just found a clue trail that needed to be followed. One that didn't lead anywhere.  I don't daytime drink.
Ah well, perhaps in the evening then.

What was all that about? Didn't you learn anything from the last time you screwed this up? If you had been open to the possibility of love when you first saw him, maybe your life wouldn't have turned out so
So what? How did my life turn out?
Why am I even bothering?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Elsewhere Granny’s Diner. Emma joins Mary Margaret and David.
Storybrooke. Present day. Elsewhere Granny’s Diner. Emma joins Mary Margaret and David.

dialog.dialog: How are you holding up?
How do you think?
:  Oh, Emma.
My condolences. So sorry I’ve missed the funeral, but I could never pass up to wake. Oh, did I miss the speeches? Shall I make one? I mean, I am, after all, responsible.
Emma, no. Too many people will get hurt.
Listen to your mother. Anyone who tries to interfere with my plan is gonna have to deal with the Dark One!
Don't come any closer.
Don't worry. I'm not here for your baby. Not today, anyway.
Then why are you here?
Now, that my cover's blown, I can finally pay a visit to my little sister.
Who the hell are you talking about?
Why you, of course, Regina.
What?
I'm an only child.
Cora lied to you, Regina. I'm your sister. Half, if you to get technical.
Why should I believe anything you say?
Oh, you shouldn't. It's a lot to swallow. This is why I brought a gift to help.
I don't want a gift from you.

But you shall have it. See, my gift to you is this sad, sad day. Use it to dig into our past, Regina. You need to learn the truth and you must believe it. And then, meet me on Main Street tonight. Say, sundown.
And then what?
Then I'll destroy you.
This isn't the Wild West.
No, dear. It's the Wicked West.  And I want everyone to be there. To see the Evil Queen lose.
I don't lose.
Neither do I. One of us is about to make history. See you tonight, sis.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Storybrooke docks. Hook and Henry enter over to a boat.
Storybrooke. Present day. Storybrooke docks. Hook and Henry enter over to a boat.

dialog.dialog: Here. Now that’ll do.
Another boat?
You don't like the sea?
No, it's not that. It's just my mom always seems to put me off to friends that have boats when she's working on a case.
Your mother didn't put you off to me. I wanted to bring you here.
Why?
You might not believe this, but your father and I were mates long before he met your mother.
Really? You knew my dad? What was he like?
Hop on board and you'll find out.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Granny’s Diner. David, Emma, Mary Margaret, Regina, and Tinker Bell attempt to make sense of Zelena's threat.
Storybrooke. Present day. Granny’s Diner. David, Emma, Mary Margaret, Regina, and Tinker Bell attempt to make sense of Zelena's threat.

dialog.dialog: And you're sure Cora never said anything about her?
I think I would remember if my mother told me she had a love child with a scarecrow.
It doesn't matter if Zelena is your sister or not. She wants you dead. Any idea why?
I’ve never even met her before today.
Doesn't mean you did something to piss her off.
You did manage to step a lot on people's toes back in our world.
Well, none of them were green.
There's that missing year. Maybe you did something to her then.
Stick to the lasagna, lady. It's starting to sound an awful lot like you're on her side.
Where are you going?
To find out exactly what this witch thinks I did to her.

scenes.sceneDesc: OZ. Past. Emerald City. Two guards allow Zelena to enter the Wizard’s audience room. At the far end of the room a curtain can be seen. Flames shoot up from the ground preventing Zelena to step any closer.

OZ. Past. Emerald City. Two guards allow Zelena to enter the Wizard’s audience room. At the far end of the room a curtain can be seen. Flames shoot up from the ground preventing Zelena to step any closer.

dialog.dialog: I’ve been expecting you.
How did you know I was coming?
I see all. I am Oz. The Great and Terrible. You've come because you wish to find your family.
Can you help me?
I will show you what you seek to know. But I warn you: It won't be easy for you.
Is that my mother?
Her name was Cora. She gave birth to you in another land. A cyclone struck and carried you to this world.
Why did she abandon me?
You couldn't give her the one thing she truly wanted. The ability to become royalty.
Who's that?
Your sister. Regina.
Wait. I’ve got a sister?
Your sister could do what your mother wished. Regina became queen.
Who's that man?
That is Rumplestiltskin. A very powerful wizard. The most powerful of all the realms. Like you, Regina has the gift of magic. But she has been unable to develop it.
Magic is a gift?
Yes. And he's trying to teach her that.
He should be teaching me. Not her. I need to meet this wizard. I'll pay whatever you desire. Just, please, tell me how to get there.
Look down.  When you click your heels together three times those slippers will take you wherever you desire.
What do you want in return?
I simply ask that you bring me something of Rumplestiltskin's. Be careful: It is one thing to wonder about your past but another to become envious of things you cannot have.

scenes.sceneDesc: The Enchanted Forest. Past. Dark Palace. Zelena is transported to Regina’s chambers. Zelena looks around the room and opens a wardrobe. Finally, she walks to the dressing table and decides to try the magical task Rumplestiltskin has given Regina. Smoothly, Zelena succeeds. She is unaware that Rumplestiltskin is watching.

The Enchanted Forest. Past. Dark Palace. Zelena is transported to Regina’s chambers. Zelena looks around the room and opens a wardrobe. Finally, she walks to the dressing table and decides to try the magical task Rumplestiltskin has given Regina. Smoothly, Zelena succeeds. She is unaware that Rumplestiltskin is watching.

dialog.dialog: Well, this is a day of surprises. I thought it would take you at least until—  You're not Regina.
No. I'm Cora's other daughter.
That's not possible.
I'm Zelena. Her first-born.
We'll see about that.  Oh, hello, dearie. A day of surprises indeed.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Mills Mausoleum Regina is looking for something. Mary Margaret is watching her nearby.
Storybrooke. Present day. Mills Mausoleum Regina is looking for something. Mary Margaret is watching her nearby.

dialog.dialog: Regina, is she telling the truth?
I don't know. I haven't found anything yet.
Are you sure Cora didn't set any traps or stuff? I don't think a sleeping curse would be good for the baby.
David, Belle and Tink are locking down main street in case this fight actually happens.
Regina, did you find something?
A letter.
What does it say?
Zelena is my sister.
Regina, what is it?
Nothing. Don't worry about it. It's my fight. I got it handled.
What the hell was in that letter?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Main street. David, Belle and Tinker Bell walk down the street.
Storybrooke. Present day. Main street. David, Belle and Tinker Bell walk down the street.

dialog.dialog: If we position someone there, there and there we'll have the whole street covered.
I'll talk to Blue. See if we can get any reinforcement.
No. Haven't you heard Zelena? She said no interference. She would have Gold level half the block if we try something before she has a little chat with her sister.
So, they really are sisters?
She found a letter in her vault confirming it.
Where is she? Regina?
She disappeared. Something in that letter upset her.
Should we try to find her?
Regina was pretty clear she didn't want any help on this one.
You'll let her walk into this fight alone?
No, she's gonna get help whether she wants it or not.
But you just said we can't interfere.
Because Zelena has Gold on her side we need to remove him from the equation. It's the only way Regina has a fighting chance. We just need to get his dagger.
You saw Zelena. She practically has it glued to her hand.
Wait. What if I can get through to Rumple without the dagger?
It's worth a try. Regina can't do this alone.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke, Present day. Somewhere in the woods. Again, Regina reads silently the letter she found in her vault. Patrolling the area an armed Robin Hood recognizes Regina and decides to approach her.

Storybrooke, Present day. Somewhere in the woods. Again, Regina reads silently the letter she found in her vault. Patrolling the area an armed Robin Hood recognizes Regina and decides to approach her.

dialog.dialog: We have to stop meeting like this.
Did the Charmings send you to give me a pep talk? Cause I don't do all the pep talk.
No, nothing of the sort. After Zelena's threat I decided to patrol the woods. In case she'd decided to build the ranks of her simian army.  How are you holding up?
I'm not a flying monkey if that's what you mean.
So, you're gonna tell me what's in that letter you've been starring at?
What letter?
This one. . I'm a pickpocket by trade. I spent many years learning how to rob from magicians.
You're lucky I'm saving my strength for that witch.
I think, deep down you actually wanna talk about what's in there.
What makes you think you know me so well?
Well, for one thing I'd be charred to a crisp by now, if you didn't.
True.
So, can I read it?
Not stopping you.

„Cora, dear. I’ve finally got my hands on your first born. Never thought I’d find her, did you? Now I know why: She’s the most powerful sorceress I’ve ever encountered. Even more powerful than you. Stunning in every way.“Rather complimentary I'd say. Why is this troubling you?
I’ve seen that letter a hundred times before. In my darkest moments I'd go to it for comfort. For solace. For brace when I needed it. Because I- Because I always thought it was about me.
It's about Zelena.
Rumplestiltskin thinks she's more powerful than I am.
Why do you care what that imp thinks?
Because, if the man who taught me everything I know about magic, thinks she is stronger than I am, then there's no way I can win this fight.

scenes.sceneDesc: The Enchanted Forest. Past. Zelena and Rumplestiltskin walk together in the woods.
The Enchanted Forest. Past. Zelena and Rumplestiltskin walk together in the woods.

dialog.dialog: Cora never mentioned me?

A first born daughter? I think that's something I would remember. Now, there's a spell, a curse I’ve been working on for a very long time. Now I foresaw that that curse can only be cast by Cora's daughter.
That's why you've been training Regina. You thought she was going to cast the curse.
Until today. Shall we see what you can do? Magic isn't about what you see, dearie, it's about what you feel inside.  You'll have to dig deep if you wanna pass tonight's test.
What am I meant to do?
Simple. Find me.  Over here, dearie.  Close, but not close enough. Magic comes from emotion. Simply think of a moment that makes you sieve with anger.
And use it to feel the magic. Yes, I’ve learned that a long time ago. It's hard to pick one. Finding out that my mother abandoned me. That my father never wanted me.
Getting warmer.
That my sister got everything I’ve ever desired. That she didn't even have to present something. It all just happened. And she doesn't even know that.
Ding Dong. There it is. I can feel it in your nails.
Sorry, I lost control.
And now, you need to think of a moment of happiness rein in some of that anger.
What do you think about?
I'm the teacher. I ask the questions.
But, if you don't tell me that how will I learn?

Well, like you, I was abandoned as a child. Some spinsters took us in. We didn't have much. But we got by. Whenever we finished a job they used to bake meat pie. The entire hovel would fill with the most wonderful smell. And after they were cold they always let me take the first bite. And as the food warmed my body that was the only time I forgot who I really was. A boy who wasn't wanted by his papa.
Mhm.  Then, I know my moment.
What's that, dearie?
The moment I stopped feeling like someone who wasn't wanted. The moment you agreed to train me.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Zelena's Farmhouse. Belle approaches the storm cellar.
Storybrooke. Present day. Zelena's Farmhouse. Belle approaches the storm cellar.

dialog.dialog: All clear. There's no sign of Zelena. It looks like she's still keeping Gold in the cellar.
Rumple?
Belle?
I’ve come to free you.
No, leave. Leave. You have no idea what this witch will make me do to you. She can't see you with me.
I'm not afraid. You would never hurt me.
It's futile. As long as she holds the dagger I can't leave.
I'm not leaving without you.
It's not worth the risk.
Just. try. You just have to believe in us.
Run!
What?
Run! Go!
Don't let be. Carry on. I was just enjoying the show.
Belle?
Over here.
It was a trick. She's toying with us.
Zelena sends a message. She will face Regina and no one interferes. Next time you try to stop her I will kill you.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. On the beach. Henry puts a rope and a book aside.
Storybrooke. Present day. On the beach. Henry puts a rope and a book aside.

dialog.dialog: I think I’ve tried every knot in here. When do we get to the part about my dad?
Just a few moments longer. There. Come here.
What's this?
That, my boy, is a sextant. It's used by sailors to navigate.
Like a GPS?
Aye. It measures our position using the stars.
Not sure, if that's what a GPS does, but what does this have to do with my dad?
I taught him to navigate with one. Just like I'm gonna teach you.
Were you both in the Navy or something?
No, Bae - Neal was just a boy when I taught him.

Wait. Aren’t you the same age? It seems like the more you guys tell me about my dad less things make sense. I don't wanna learn how to navigate. I just wanna hear something about him that doesn't make it sound like it's made up so I like this guy.
Your mother isn't keeping things from you because she doesn't want you to know the truth.
I know she's doing it to protect me. But how am I supposed to feel anything about him being gone if I don't know what he was like when he was here?
Fair enough, mate. What I haven't told you is why I taught your father to sail. Because he'd just lost his father. I thought the sea would help ease his sorrow.
Wait. He lost his dad, too?
Aye. To something dark and evil. When he wasn't much older than you.
Really?
Indeed. You see, you might not think you know much about your father but you have more in common with him than you realize.

scenes.sceneDesc: The Enchanted Forest. Past. The Dark Castle. Zelena arranges a meat pie on the table. Rumplestiltskin enters the room.
The Enchanted Forest. Past. The Dark Castle. Zelena arranges a meat pie on the table. Rumplestiltskin enters the room.

dialog.dialog: I don't remember giving permission for guests.
That's for you. Meat pie. Just like the spinsters used to make when you were a boy.
Can't stay. Have a lesson to teach.
But, we just finished a lesson.
Not with you, dearie. With Regina.
You're still training her?
Did you think I was gonna stop?
Wait. You don't need Regina. I'm going to cast your curse.  She's not nearly as powerful as me. She doesn't deserve to have you teaching her.
Careful, Zelena. What was it your father used to always tell you?
No matter, what you feel on the inside you always have to put on a good face.
You might wanna take his advice. Your inside is starting to show.  You're turning green, dearie. Envy will do this to you. And don't wait up. This could take a while.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Main street. Citizens assemble to watch the upcoming fight.
Storybrooke. Present day. Main street. Citizens assemble to watch the upcoming fight.

dialog.dialog: Have you seen Regina?
Not since the vault.
If she hasn't get her soon that witch will take it out on all of us.
Let's take everybody out of here before that happens.  Alright, listen up. We need you to get back to your home.
No one's going anywhere.  This show needs an audience.  Out of my way, munchkin.
I'm a dwarf.
That's even worse. Where is she? Don't tell me. She's a coward. This isn't good. For all of you. If my sister isn't here in five minutes I'm going to let the Dark One of his leash.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Main street. Five minutes later.
Storybrooke. Present day. Main street. Five minutes later.

dialog.dialog: Time's up. Who'd you wanna kill first, Rumple?
He's not killing anyone. If you wanna fight someone, Zelena, fight me.
Sorry dear, I don't dance with amateurs.
I'm not an amateur. I'm the savior.
Looks like someone has an inflated sense of self-worth.  Anybody else wanna give it a go?
I do.  Didn't anyone tell you? Black is my color.
But it looks so much better on me. I was beginning to think you aren't going to show up.
I couldn't let my sister off that easily.
So, you've finally accepted me into the family?
I’ve accepted that we shared a mother, yes. But I still have one question: What the hell did I ever do to you?
Isn't it obvious? You were born.
I’ve been waiting to do that. All day.
Rumplestiltskin can't save you this time.

scenes.sceneDesc: Past. Enchanted Forest. Dark Castle. Regina is sitting at her dressing table. She combs her hair. Her father’s razor in hand Zelena approaches her from behind.
Past. Enchanted Forest. Dark Castle. Regina is sitting at her dressing table. She combs her hair. Her father’s razor in hand Zelena approaches her from behind.

dialog.dialog: Don't make a sound. Rumplestiltskin is wasting his time with you. You don't deserve him. You don't deserve any of this. Our mother couldn't see and neither can he. But I'll show them they're wrong.
Surprise, dearie.  As I always said jealousy drives people to do crazy things.
You tricked me.
Consider it a test. One you failed.
I was just trying to make your decision easier. Show you that I'm all powerful. That I'm the one who's going to cast your curse.
I appreciate your efforts, dearie, but I'm afraid you just disqualified yourself.
What? Why?
Because casting the curse has a price. A steep one. You have to give up the thing you love most.
I can do that.
That's the problem, dearie. The thing you love most is me.
You think I love you?
I'm a perceptive soul.
You're insane.
Besides the point. Dearie, it's okay. I know, I have that effect on women. You love me and that, dearie, makes you too dangerous.
So, Regina
has the job.  You might wanna get that checked.
Forget the curse. I'll find another way to give you what you want.
Unless you can take me to a land without magic I'm afraid that's not possible.
I can. I could have taken you there.
How?
These slippers. The Wizard gave them to me and they can take you anywhere you want to go.
Wizard?
But it's too late.
Who said it's too late?
You chose her.
Perhaps it was haste.
I'm not naive. There's only one way you'll ever get these slippers from me. You'll have to kill me.
Well, if I must.
You shouldn't have taught me all your tricks, Rumple. I'll see you again, dearie. Next time, you will choose me.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Main Street. Mr. Gold standing between them Zelena faces Regina.
Storybrooke. Present day. Main Street. Mr. Gold standing between them Zelena faces Regina.

dialog.dialog: He should’ve chosen me.
Who?
Rumplestiltskin.
That's what this is about? You're jealous of me?
You still don't realize what you had. You never did. You got everything I ever wanted and you didn't even deserve it. But I'm gonna take it all from you.
My Miata.
You can't beat me, little sis. Everything Rumplestiltskin taught you, he taught me, too. But I was the better student.
: What are you waiting for? Kill me.
I never said I wanted to kill you. I said I wanted to destroy you. And to do that I need your heart.  Where is it?
My mother taught me one thing: Never bring your heart to a witch fight. Something you'd know if she hadn't abandoned you.
You haven't won, Regina. I will get your heart. I will get everything you ever had.
Not today.
Are you alright?
I'm still alive, aren't I?
Gold disappeared. We thought that meant she
defeated me? Hardly.
You've won?
Don't act so surprised. As it turns out Zelena wanted my heart. It's a good thing I wasn't stupid enough to bring it with me.
Any idea why she wants it?
Well, she got your courage and wants my heart. Those are ingredients.
For what? A curse?
We’re already in Storybrooke. We’ve already lost our memories. What else could she do to us?

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Somewhere in the woods. Hurriedly Regina runs into the forest. Pointing a flashlight here and there she’s looking for something.
Storybrooke. Present day. Somewhere in the woods. Hurriedly Regina runs into the forest. Pointing a flashlight here and there she’s looking for something.

dialog.dialog: Did the plan work?
That depends - is it still here?
Right, where you left it.
Then it worked. I just needed to find the one thing I had that Zelena didn't.
And what exactly was that?
My heartless mother.  My sister was right about something. She said I don't always realize what I have right in front of me.
What's that?
Just, that I didn't always appreciate things.  Would you mind holding on to this a little bit longer?
You're really going to entrust something so valuable to a common thief like me?
You can't steal something that's been given to you.
You still owe me that drink.
Yes, I suppose, I do.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. The Blanchard loft.
Storybrooke. Present day. The Blanchard loft.

dialog.dialog: Thanks, Kilian. Maybe we can go out on your boat again sometime.
Anytime, lad.
Hey.
How did the Queen fare against the Witch?
She survived. But the rest is definitely more than a doorway conversation.  Thanks for taking him.
There's more Bae in your boy than you realize. He needs to know about his father, Emma. You can't just take him back to New York when this is over and pretend like none of this is real.
Thanks again.

scenes.sceneDesc: Storybrooke. Present day. Zelena's Farmhouse. Zelena locks Mr. Gold up.
Storybrooke. Present day. Zelena's Farmhouse. Zelena locks Mr. Gold up.

dialog.dialog: What, no meat pie?
She's clever, Rumple. But, so am I. Regina's heart is somewhere in this town. It's only a matter of time before I find it.
Whatever twisted curse you have planned, it won't change anything. If I had to do it all again, I'd still choose Regina.
Would you, now? Well, I suppose we'll just have to wait and see.
I'm afraid, I don't understand.
We are doing it all over again. What I'm casting isn't a curse.  It's a second chance.

scenes.sceneDesc: Oz. Past. The Emerald City. Two guards try to prevent Zelena from entering the Wizard’s audience room. Using magic she flings both of them aside and enters.
Oz. Past. The Emerald City. Two guards try to prevent Zelena from entering the Wizard’s audience room. Using magic she flings both of them aside and enters.

dialog.dialog: I learned some tricks while I was gone. You showed me my past. Now take me there. To the moment my mother abandoned me. That's when it all went wrong. I need to go back and change it.
You want to travel back in time? To change the past?
What's so funny?
I'm afraid that's impossible.
I thought, you were the Great and Terrible Oz.
Even my power has its limits.Zelena:

dialog.character: (she rippes down the curtain.) You're telling me. (dragging the Wizard towards herself.)

dialog.dialog: No. Don't hurt me. Please.
Who are you?
My name is Walsh. I'm just a circus huckster. I'm a showman. I come from a distant land. A place called Kansas.
You're not even a wizard?
It's all part of the act. But, what I peddle is real. What you saw there is real . Those slippers  took you to the Enchanted Forest, didn't they? I may not have magic. But the things I collect do.
That's why you wanted me to bring you something of Rumplestiltskin's. So, you could carry on this charade.
I didn't mean any harm. I was just trying to help us both. What you seek doesn't exist. No magic is that strong.
Well, then I have no use for you. At least not like that.
Like what?
What I need is someone willing to do whatever I ask without question.  Like a trained circus animal.
You're evil.
Not evil, dear. Wicked.
No. No.
Much better. Now, come.  I'm going to find a way to change the past. I’ve got work to do.
We'll see about that. When I'm done, Regina, you'll never even been born.
